steps.title: Use a two-point exponential fit
steps.content: "170% more" means the growth factor is $1+1.70=2.70$. Since the end-of-2016 count is $216$ and $t=1$ then, the implied $t=0$ value is $216/2.7=80$. Enter $x_1=[0,1]$, $y_1=[216/2.7,216]$, and $y_1\sim a b^{x_1}$.

steps.title: Read the model
steps.content: Desmos gives $a=80$ and $b=2.7$, so the equation is $n=80(2.7)^t$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Verify by checking $t=1$: $80\times2.7=216$. Choice B has the correct starting value and growth factor, so B is correct.
choices: • Choice A: $n = 80(1.7)^t$ — uses a growth factor of $1.7$, which would represent a 70% increase, not 170% more. Eliminated.
• Choice B: $n = 80(2.7)^t$ — growth factor $2.7$ correctly reflects 170% more, and $n_0 = 80$ is confirmed by back-solving with $n = 216$ at $t = 1$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
• Choice C: $n = 216(1.7)^t$ — wrong growth factor and uses the year-2016 count as the starting value, shifting the timeline. Eliminated.
• Choice D: $n = 216(2.7)^t$ — correct factor but wrong initial value; this would give $n_0 = 216$ at $t = 0$ (end of 2015), contradicting the problem. Eliminated.
